This phenomenon happens in multi-story buildings when the main water supply is shut off for maintenance or repairs. As the water drains out of the vertical pipes toward the lower floors, it creates a powerful vacuum in the upper sections of the plumbing system. Because there is no more water pressure pushing out, the pipes act like a giant straw. When you open your faucet, the high atmospheric pressure outside forces air and nearby liquids into the tap to fill that empty space.

mentions.description:

mentions.title: Why the Size of Your Water Softener Matters | Plumbing Tips
mentions.description:
When it comes to water softeners in Texas, bigger isn't always better. Michael Marugo, Plumbing Operations Manager at ABC Home & Commercial Services, explains why getting the right size matters.

An oversized water softener won't regenerate often enough, which means your water can go from soft to hard between cycles. An undersized one will work too hard, regenerate too frequently and wear out faster than it should.
